method: AWS Pricing Calculator for ECS Fargate architecture (region us-east-1, matches deploy/task-definition.json + deployment-contract.md)

ECS Fargate.unit: 0.5 vCPU, 1 GB RAM x 2 tasks, 24/7 (matches task-definition.json cpu=512/memory=1024, min 2 tasks)

API_Gateway_private.unit: private REST API with AWS_IAM authorization (enforces SigV4 at the edge, in front of the ALB)
API_Gateway_private.cost: $0 (per-request pricing; negligible at demo scale, within free tier)

S3_audit_and_baselines.unit: audit objects (KMS-encrypted, 1 per decision) + per-service baseline JSON

conclusion: Using ECS Fargate is cost-effective (~$53/mo) and fits comfortably within the $200/month cap. Audit + baselines are stored in S3 (KMS-encrypted), consistent with app/audit.py and the Deployment Contract; there is no DynamoDB in the deployed engine. Inference token cost is $0 (statistical model, no LLM).
